title: All-In Merchant Cost by Platform (% of Revenue)
subtitle: Traffic/advertising costs dominate on Taobao/Tmall — commissions are the distraction
notes: Taobao/Tmall commission includes 0.6% base software fee + 2-5% Tmall commission. PDD 'best' = price-competitive merchants getting algorithmic traffic. JD take rate of ~2.4% reflects 1P/3P mix. Douyin estimates based on ~10% all-in with subsidies.

title: Food Delivery War: Meituan Profit Swing and Market Share Shift
subtitle: Meituan swung from RMB 35.8B profit to RMB 24.3B loss in one year
notes: Meituan FY2024 net income from annual results. Q1-Q3 figures are quarterly operating results. Market share figures are estimates from UBS and industry analysts. Meituan FY2025E net loss of 23.3-24.3B yuan per Caixin (Feb 2026).

title: Heimao Complaint Response Rate by Platform (Double 11 2025)
subtitle: Taobao responds to fewer than 1 in 10 consumer complaints
notes: Response rate = percentage of consumer complaints on Heimao that received a response from the platform. Data from Heimao Double 11 2025 report (Oct 9 - Nov 14) and December 2025 monthly report. Taobao/Tmall <10% rounded to 8% for visualization. JD <30% rounded to 28%. Douyin and smaller platforms at ~99%.

title: Douyin Shelf Commerce as % of Total GMV (2023-2025)
subtitle: Three-year plan achieved on schedule: 30% → 40% → 50%
notes: Douyin's 3-year plan for shelf e-commerce: 30% year 1 (2023, achieved), 40% year 2 (2024, achieved), 50% year 3 (2025, achieved by 618). Shelf GMV grew 49% YoY vs 34% overall. Influencer share declining as merchant self-hosted streams grow. H1/H2 splits are estimates based on available data points.

title: Temu US DAU/MAU Recovery Trajectory After De Minimis Elimination
subtitle: 85% parcel volume drop, 58% DAU drop — then partial recovery
notes: US DAU dropped 52-58% in May 2025 (from ~70M to ~30M). US MAU dropped 51% to 40.2M between March and June. Recovery to 60-70% of peak by July (Prime Day). October US MAU at 133.6M (still -28% YoY). Global MAU hit record 530M in August 2025 despite US decline. 90% of global MAU from outside US per HSBC. Monthly figures are interpolated from available data points.
